data.title: As a developer using /dso:brainstorm, Phase 1 Gate loops back to Tell Me More on intent gaps with bounded termination
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Amend the Phase 1 Gate in `plugins/dso/skills/brainstorm/SKILL.md` so that Gate Step 2 (Intent Gap Analysis) and the UX probe checkpoint (from S2) both loop back to the Tell Me More loop whenever an intent gap surfaces that genuinely increases epic understanding. Termination requires BOTH an empty gap list AND the user confirming the Understanding Summary in the same turn. No numeric cap is applied; the anti-redundancy directive (S4) bounds the loop in sparse codebases. Define explicit termination when the anti-redundancy directive suppresses every candidate question: record UNRESOLVABLE_INTENT_GAP on the epic and terminate instead of looping indefinitely.

**Why**: Without loop-back discipline, intent gaps surfaced by the UX probe set are swallowed and Phase 1 terminates prematurely, defeating the purpose of the probe set. The loop-back must consume the UX probe checkpoint by a named symbol (not by hardcoded probe text) so the follow-on epic aaa1-7100 can swap the probe set content without rewriting the state machine.

**Scope**:
- IN:
  - Amend Phase 1 Gate in `plugins/dso/skills/brainstorm/SKILL.md` so Step 2 and the UX probe checkpoint loop back to Tell Me More on intent gap.
  - Update Phase 1 termination condition: empty gap list AND same-turn Understanding Summary confirmation; no numeric cap.
  - Reference the UX probe checkpoint by a named symbol defined in S2's structured probe set, not by hardcoded probe content.
  - Define termination for the "all candidate questions suppressed by anti-redundancy" case: record UNRESOLVABLE_INTENT_GAP on the epic and terminate.
  - Regression test(s) exercising (a) loop-back on surfaced gap, (b) empty-gap-list + same-turn confirmation terminates Phase 1, (c) anti-redundancy suppresses all candidates → UNRESOLVABLE_INTENT_GAP recorded + termination.
- OUT:
  - The UX probe set itself and its structured data (S2).
  - The anti-redundancy directive definition (S4).
  - Non-interactive mode bypass (S5).
  - E2E dogfooding test (S6).
  - Documentation updates (S7).

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, Phase 1 Gate Step 2 (Intent Gap Analysis) loops back to Tell Me More whenever an intent gap surfaces that genuinely increases epic understanding.
  ← Satisfies: "Phase 1 Gate Step 2 (Intent Gap Analysis) and the UX probe checkpoint both loop back to Tell Me More whenever an intent gap surfaces"
- When this story is complete, the UX probe checkpoint loops back to Tell Me More whenever an intent gap surfaces that genuinely increases epic understanding.
  ← Satisfies: "Phase 1 Gate Step 2 … and the UX probe checkpoint both loop back to Tell Me More"
- When this story is complete, Phase 1 termination requires both (a) an empty gap list and (b) user confirmation of the Understanding Summary in the same turn, with no numeric cap.
  ← Satisfies: "Termination requires that the gap analysis produces an empty gap list AND the user confirms the Understanding Summary in the same turn. No numeric cap."
- When this story is complete, the loop-back state machine consumes the UX probe checkpoint by a named symbol defined in S2's structured probe set (not by hardcoded reference to specific probe content), preserving forward-compatibility with follow-on epic aaa1-7100.
- When this story is complete, when the anti-redundancy directive (S4) suppresses every candidate question during loop-back, the state machine records UNRESOLVABLE_INTENT_GAP on the epic and terminates, instead of looping indefinitely; a regression test covers this case.
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic.

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Loop-back is unbounded in sparse codebases by design; termination discipline (empty-gap-list + same-turn confirmation) plus the anti-redundancy directive from S4 and the UNRESOLVABLE_INTENT_GAP escape hatch prevent runaway dialogues.
- [Maintainability] The Phase 1 Gate amendment must preserve the existing structure (Step 1 — Understanding Summary → Step 2 — Intent Gap Analysis → proceed to Phase 2) so downstream consumers that parse the SKILL.md gate structure continue to work.

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
